2.1.description: General standards for milk and milk products, including definitions, usage of dairy terms, and specific product standards.

2.1.1.category_name: General Standards for Milk and Milk Products
2.1.1.description: Over-arching definitions for milk and milk products and guidance on the use of dairy terms.

2.1.2.description: Standards applying to species identified milk, mixed milk, and standardized milk variants like full cream or toned milk.

2.1.3.description: Product prepared from milk or milk derivatives and edible flavourings with or without sweeteners and other non-dairy ingredients.

2.1.4.category_name: Standard for Evaporated or Concentrated Milk
2.1.4.description: Product obtained by partial removal of water from milk by heat or other processes to achieve specific composition.

2.1.5.description: Product obtained by partial removal of water from milk with the addition of sugar.

2.1.6.description: Product obtained by partial removal of water from any variant of milk with or without added milk solids by heating.

2.1.7.description: Fluid product comparatively rich in fat obtained from milk or by reconstitution or recombination of milk products.

2.1.8.description: Fatty products derived exclusively from milk including ghee, butter oil, and anhydrous milk fat.

2.1.9.description: Fatty product principally in the form of a water-in-oil emulsion derived exclusively from milk or milk products.

2.1.10.category_name: Standard for Milk Powders and Cream Powder
2.1.10.description: Milk products obtained by partial removal of water from milk or cream.

2.1.11.description: Product prepared from milk containing added carbohydrates such as sucrose, dextrose, and maltodextrin.

2.1.12.description: Milk products obtained by drying Whey or Acid Whey.

2.1.13.description: Products obtained by fermentation of milk resulting in lowering of pH with or without coagulation, including Dahi and Yoghurt.

2.1.14.category_name: Standard for Ice Cream, Kulfi, Chocolate Ice Cream, Softy Ice-Cream, Milk Ice, Milk Lolly and Dried Ice Cream Mix
2.1.14.description: Frozen milk products obtained by freezing a pasteurized mix prepared from milk or milk products and permitted ingredients.

2.1.15.category_name: Standard for Frozen Desserts or Confections with Added Vegetable Oil/ Fat or Vegetable Protein
2.1.15.description: Product obtained by freezing a pasteurised mix prepared with edible vegetable oils, fats, or vegetable protein.

2.1.16.description: Product obtained from any variant of milk by precipitation with permitted acidulants and heating.

2.1.17.category_name: Standard for Cheese and Cheese Products
2.1.17.description: Ripened or unripened product obtained by coagulating the protein of milk or milk products.

2.1.18.description: Products obtained by separating, washing and drying the coagulum of skimmed milk.

2.1.20.description: White to light yellow crystalline, slightly sweet disaccharide sugar found in milk.

2.1.21.description: Complex milk proteins containing casein and whey protein in their native form manufactured by removing lactose and minerals.

2.1.22.description: Product obtained by removing nonprotein constituents from whey followed by drying.

2.1.23.category_name: Standard for Cow or Buffalo Colostrum and Colostrum products
2.1.23.description: Lacteal secretion from the mammary glands of cow or buffalo obtained upto three to five days of parturition.

2.1.24.description: Dried milk product characterized by a high content of lactose manufactured from permeates.

2.2.description: Standards for various categories of fats, oils, and fat emulsions including refined oils, hydrogenated fats, and spreads.

2.2.1.description: Standardized edible oils extracted from various vegetable sources.

2.2.1.1.description: Oil expressed from copra obtained from the kernel of Cocos mucifera nuts.

2.2.1.1.1.description: Oil expressed from the kernel of Cocos nucifera nuts by mechanical or natural means without heat alteration.

2.2.1.3.description: Oil extracted from clean, sound delinted and decorticated cotton seeds.

2.2.1.4.description: Oil expressed from clean and sound groundnuts.

2.2.1.5.description: Oil obtained by process of expressing clean and sound Flaxseed or Linseed.

2.2.1.5a.description: Oil expressed from clean and sound seeds or nuts of Madhuca.

2.2.1.6.description: Oil obtained from juncea or napus varieties of Brassica.

2.2.1.7.category_name: Rapeseed or mustard oil-low erucic acid
2.2.1.7.description: Oil obtained from low erucic acid bearing seeds of rapeseed belonging to Brassica varieties.

2.2.1.8.description: Oil obtained solely from the fruit of the olive tree (Olea europaea L.).

2.2.1.9.description: Oil expressed from poppy seeds (papaver somniferum).

2.2.1.10.description: Oil expressed from the seeds of Carthamus tinctorius.

2.2.1.10.1.category_name: Imported Safflower seed oil and High Oleic Safflower seed oil
2.2.1.10.1.description: Oil expressed from Safflower seeds containing not less than 70% oleic acid.

2.2.1.12.description: Oil expressed from clean and sound seeds of Til (Sesamum indicum).

2.2.1.14.description: Oil expressed from clean and sound soyabeans.

2.2.1.15.description: Oil extracted from the germ of clean and sound seeds of zea mays linn.

2.2.1.16.description: Vegetable oil obtained by expression or solvent extraction that has been deacidified, bleached, and deodorised.

2.2.1.17.description: Oil expressed from the seeds of sweet or bitter almond without heat.

2.2.1.18.description: Oil extracted from the clean, sound seeds of the fruit of water-melon.

2.2.1.19.description: Oil obtained from fleshy mesocarp of fruits of the oil palm.

2.2.1.20.1.description: Liquid fraction obtained by fractionation of palm oil.

2.2.1.21.description: Oil obtained from sound kernel of the fruits of oil palm.

2.2.1.22.description: Oil obtained from clean and sound sunflower seeds or cake.

2.2.1.22.01.description: Oil obtained from high oleic acid bearing sunflower seeds containing not less than 75% oleic acid.

2.2.1.23.description: Oil obtained from the layer around the endosperm of rice.

2.2.1.24.description: Admixture of any two edible vegetable oils.

2.2.1.25.description: Oil obtained from the avocado fruit.

2.2.1.26.description: High melting fraction obtained by fractionation of palm oil.

2.2.1.27.description: Solid fraction obtained by fractionation of palm kernel oil.

2.2.1.28.description: Liquid fraction obtained by fractionation of Palm Kernel oil.

2.2.1.29.description: Liquid fraction obtained by fractionation of Palm oil.

2.2.1.30.description: Oil expressed from the clean and sound seeds of chia.

2.2.1.31.description: Oil expressed from the clean and sound Grape seeds.

2.2.1.32.description: Oil obtained by the process of expressing clean and sound Moringa Seed.

2.2.2.description: Edible fatty material treated to rearrange fatty acid positions within the glyceride entities.

2.2.3.description: Soyabean oil subjected to partial hydrogenation and optional winterization.

2.2.3.1.category_name: Partially hydrogenated and winterised soyabean oil
2.2.3.1.description: Deodourised product obtained by light hydrogenation and winterization of soyabean oil.

2.2.3.2.description: Deodourised product obtained by light hydrogenation of soyabean oil.

2.2.4.description: Various animal and vegetable fats and butters.

2.2.4.1.description: Fat obtained from a beef carcass.

2.2.4.2.description: Fat obtained from the carcass of sheep.

2.2.4.5.description: Fat produced by extraction or expression from cocoa beans.

2.2.4.6.description: Fat obtained from seed kernels of sal trees.

2.2.4.7.description: Fat obtained from clean and sound kernels of Kokum.

2.2.4.8.description: Fat obtained from clean and sound kernels of Mango.

2.2.4.9.description: Fat obtained from clean and sound seed kernels of Dhupa.

2.2.4.10.description: Fat obtained from clean and sound seed kernels of Phulwara.

2.2.4.11.description: Cohesive, comminuted food product prepared from clean, sound, shelled peanuts or groundnuts.

2.2.4.12.description: Product obtained by pressing and extraction from shea kernels derived from Shea tree.

2.2.4.13.description: Fat obtained by pressing and extraction from Illipe seeds.

2.2.5.description: Emulsions of edible oils and fats with water.

2.2.5.1.description: Emulsion of edible oils and fats with water.

2.2.5.2.description: Emulsion of vegetable oil product with water meant for bakery/industrial use.

2.2.5.3.description: Product in the form of water in oil emulsion containing milk fat or vegetable fat.

2.2.6.1.description: Refined edible vegetable oil subjected to hydrogenation.

2.2.6.2.description: Vanaspati meant for use as a shortening or leavening agent.

2.2.9.description: Oils obtained by solvent extraction using food grade hexane, not for direct human consumption.

2.3.description: General category for processed and preserved fruit and vegetable products.

2.3.1.description: Products obtained from sound, matured, dehydrated, fresh or frozen fruits packed with any suitable packing medium and processed by heat.

2.3.2.description: Product prepared from a mixture of fruits, packed with water or other suitable liquid packing medium and processed by heat.

2.3.3.description: Product obtained from fresh, dehydrated or frozen vegetables either singly or in combination, packed with suitable packing medium and processed by heat.

2.3.3A.description: Prepared from selected, fresh, washed, cleaned, firm and ripe tomatoes of variety Lycopersicum esculentum Mill.

2.3.4.category_name: Thermally Processed Curried Vegetables / Ready to Eat Vegetables
2.3.4.description: Product prepared from fresh, dehydrated or frozen or previously processed vegetables, legumes, cereals or pulses, cooked with spices.

2.3.5.description: Prepared from juice/pulp/puree of sound, mature vegetables, blended with ingredients and cooked to a suitable consistency.

2.3.6.description: Unfermented but fermentable product, pulpy, turbid or clear, obtained from sound, ripe fruit and processed by heat or mechanical means.

2.3.7.description: Unfermented but fermentable product intended for direct consumption obtained from the edible part of vegetables.

2.3.8.description: Unfermented juice obtained from tomatoes processed by heat, free from extraneous plant material.

2.3.9.description: Unfermented but fermentable pulpy or non-pulpy product made from fruit juice/pulp/puree with water and sweeteners.

2.3.10.category_name: Thermally Processed Fruit Beverages / Fruit Drink/ Ready to Serve Fruit Beverages
2.3.10.description: Unfermented but fermentable product prepared from juice or pulp/puree or concentrated juice or pulp of sound mature fruit.

2.3.11.category_name: Thermally Processed Mango Pulp / Puree and Sweetened Mango Pulp / Puree
2.3.11.description: Unfermented but fermentable product obtained from edible portion of sound, ripe mangoes.

2.3.12.category_name: Thermally Processed Fruit Pulp / Puree And Sweetened Fruit Pulp / Puree other than Mango
2.3.12.description: Unfermented but fermentable product obtained from edible portion of sound, ripe fruit other than mango.

2.3.13.category_name: Thermally Processed Concentrated Fruit / Vegetable Juice Pulp/ Puree
2.3.13.description: Unfermented product obtained from juice/pulp/puree of fruit/vegetable from which water has been removed.

2.3.14.category_name: Thermally Processed Tomato Puree and Paste
2.3.14.description: Unfermented product capable of fermentation, obtained by concentrating the juice of sound ripe tomatoes.

2.3.15.description: Products obtained by mechanical dehydration of fresh vegetables/fruits juice/pulp/puree blended with ingredients.

2.3.16.category_name: Fruit/Vegetable Juice / Pulp/ Puree with Preservatives for Industrial Use only
2.3.16.description: Unfermented but fermentable product obtained by mechanical process from sound ripe fruits/vegetables.

2.3.17.category_name: Concentrated Fruit Vegetable Juice /Pulp / Puree with Preservatives for Industrial Use Only
2.3.17.description: Unfermented product obtained from juice/pulp/puree of fruit/vegetable from which water has been removed.

2.3.18.description: Unfermented product obtained from fresh or dried tamarind by boiling with water and sieving.

2.3.19.description: Product prepared by blending fruit pulp/puree with sweeteners and fat, dehydrated to form a sheet.

2.3.20.description: Product prepared by blending fruit/vegetable pulp/puree with starch, cereals and sweeteners, dehydrated into flakes.

2.3.21.category_name: Squashes, Crushes, Fruit Syrups/Fruit Sharbats and Barley Water
2.3.21.description: Product prepared from fruit juice/puree/concentrate blended with nutritive sweeteners and water.

2.3.22.description: Product prepared by blending ginger juice or its oleoresin or essence with water and nutritive sweeteners.

2.3.23.category_name: Synthetic Syrup for use in Dispensers for carbonated water
2.3.23.description: Carbonated water obtained by blending nutritive sweeteners with water and other ingredients.

2.3.24.description: Syrup obtained by blending syrup made from sugar, dextrose or liquid glucose.

2.3.25.description: Product prepared from fruits, rhizome or vegetables impregnated with nutritive sweeteners to preserve it.

2.3.26.description: Product prepared from fruits, vegetables, rhizomes or peel impregnated with nutritive sweeteners, optionally coated with sugar or syrup.

2.3.27.description: Product prepared by blending tomato juice/puree/paste with nutritive sweeteners, salt, vinegar, spices and condiments.

2.3.28.description: Culinary preparation prepared from edible portion of fruit/vegetable blended with nutritive sweeteners, salt, spices and condiments.

2.3.29.description: Product obtained from wholesome soybeans by fermenting the soybean paste and blending with salt and nutritive sweeteners.

2.3.30.category_name: Carbonated Fruit Beverages or Fruit Drinks

2.3.31.description: Product prepared from fruit ingredients mixed with nutritive sweetener and processed to a suitable consistency.

2.3.33.description: Product prepared from pulp/puree of sound, ripe fruit(s) by cooking with salt and nutritive sweeteners to attain a thick consistency.

2.3.35.description: Product prepared from edible part of suitable variety of sound fruit from which moisture has been removed.

2.3.39.description: Product prepared from vegetables, legumes, cereals or pulses, cooked with spices and frozen.

2.3.40.description: Product in powder form intended for use after dilution, obtained by blending fruit solids with nutritive sweeteners.

2.3.59.description: Food products produced by the reduction or removal of non-protein constituents from vegetable materials.

2.5.description: Standards for various meat, poultry, and egg products including processing definitions and quality requirements.

2.5.1.description: Definitions of terms used in the standards such as animal, carcass, meat, edible offal, and slaughter.

2.5.2.9.category_name: Fresh or Chilled or Frozen Chevon or Goat Meat
2.5.2.9.description: Raw chevon including whole carcasses, pieces, cuts, or edible offal packed in suitable material.

2.5.2.10.category_name: Fresh or Chilled or Frozen Mutton or Sheep Meat
2.5.2.10.description: Raw mutton including whole carcasses, pieces, cuts, or edible offal packed in suitable material.

2.5.2.11.category_name: Fresh or Chilled or Frozen Poultry Meat
2.5.2.11.description: Poultry meat including whole carcasses, pieces, cuts, or edible offal packed in suitable material.

2.5.2.12.description: Meat products treated with a marinade to improve colour, flavor, tenderness, and yield.

2.5.2.13.description: Meat products preserved by microbial fermentation which lowers pH and water activity.

2.5.2.14.description: Soft cylindrical containers obtained from intestines, oesophagus, and urinary bladder of food animals.

2.5.3.description: Standards for fresh eggs and processed egg products.

2.5.3.1.description: Eggs in shell other than broken, incubated, or cooked eggs.

2.5.3.2.description: Pasteurized and frozen whole egg, egg yolk, or egg white prepared from hens' eggs.

2.5.3.3.description: Product obtained by suitable drying of liquid contents of sound hens' eggs.

2.5.3.4.description: Homogeneous product from hens' eggs that is pasteurized and preserved in liquid form.

2.5.3.5.description: Cooked eggs preserved using vinegar, oil, and salt as major pickling ingredients.

2.6.description: General standards and regulations concerning fish, crustaceans, molluscs, and derived products.

2.6.1.description: Specific product standards for various frozen, canned, and processed fish and seafood items.

2.6.1.1.description: Frozen product obtained from shrimp species, peeled or unpeeled, raw or cooked.

2.6.1.2.description: Product prepared from fresh lobsters of sound quality belonging to specified families.

2.6.1.3.category_name: Frozen squid and parts of squid
2.6.1.3.description: Product prepared from fresh squid of sound quality belonging to specified squid families.

2.6.1.4.description: Product frozen from finfish species suitable for human consumption, with or without head and viscera.

2.6.1.5.description: Slices of fish removed from the carcass of species suitable for human consumption, frozen.

2.6.1.7.description: Product prepared from fresh or wholesome fish after drying with or without addition of salt.

2.6.1.8.description: Canned finfish, crustaceans and molluscs solid packed or packed in suitable medium.

2.6.1.9.description: Frozen product prepared from sound squid, cuttlefish or octopus offered for direct consumption.

2.6.1.10.description: Fish product that is smoked, smoke-flavoured, or smoke-dried.

2.6.1.11.category_name: Ready-to-Eat Finfish or Shell Fish Curry in Retortable Pouches
2.6.1.11.description: Thermal processed instant fish or shell fish curry packed in retortable pouches.

2.6.1.12.description: Oils intended for human consumption derived from fresh and frozen fish, shellfish and/or their parts.

2.6.1.13.description: Fine powder prepared from non-oily white fish like sprats.

2.6.1.14.description: Fermented or preserved fish product with spices and oil.

2.6.1.15.description: Frozen meat separated from clean, fresh, gutted fish.

2.6.1.16.description: Freeze dried product prepared from clean, wholesome and fresh prawns.

2.6.1.17.description: Frozen meat picked from Vallarta, Meretrix, or other edible clam species.

2.6.1.18.description: Bivalve molluscs that are alive immediately prior to consumption or processed raw.

2.6.1.19.description: Granular product prepared from fish eggs of the Acipenseridae family.

2.6.1.20.description: Translucent liquid condiment produced by fermentation of fish and salt.

2.6.1.21.category_name: Quick Frozen Fish Sticks (fish fingers), Fish Portions and Fish Fillets Breaded or Battered
2.6.1.21.description: Quick frozen fish products coated with breading or batter.

2.6.1.22.category_name: Fresh and Quick Frozen Raw Scallop Products
2.6.1.22.description: Scallop adductor muscle meat with or without roe from the Pectinidae family.

2.6.1.23.description: Fish mince based emulsion product stuffed into casing and pasteurized.

2.6.1.24.description: Ready-to-eat product from crab meat that has been cooked, pasteurized and chilled.

2.6.1.25.description: Gelatin derived from collagen found in fish processing waste.

2.6.2.description: Regulations specifying limits for naturally occurring formaldehyde in fish species.

2.7.description: Category covering sugar boiled confectionery, lozenges, chewing gum, chocolate, and cocoa products.

2.7.1.description: Processed composite food article made from sugar with or without doctoring agents by process of boiling.
ingredients: spices and condiments and their extracts

2.7.2.description: Confections made mainly out of pulverised sugar or icing sugar with binding materials generally made from cold mixing.

2.7.3.description: Products prepared from chewing gum base or bubble gum base, natural or synthetic, non-toxic, with cane sugar and liquid glucose.
ingredients: Glycerol ester or partially hydrogenated gum or wood rosin

2.7.4.description: Homogeneous product obtained from cocoa materials including beans, nib, mass, cake, and powder with or without sugars and milk solids.
ingredients: spices and condiments and their extracts

2.7.5.description: Frozen ice produce which contains citric acid, permitted flavours and colours, and sometimes fruits or nuts.

2.7.6.category_name: Dry Mixtures of Cocoa and Sugars
2.7.6.description: Product obtained from Cocoa Cake transformed into powder mixed with sugars intended for direct consumption.

2.7.7.description: Material obtained by mechanical transformation from the partial removal of fat from the ground nib of well-fermented sound roasted beans.
ingredients: roasted beans of Theobroma cacao L

2.7.8.category_name: Cocoa mass or Cocoa/Chocolate Liquor and Cocoa Cake
2.7.8.description: Product prepared by grinding clean, practically shell-free cocoa nibs, or the product obtained by removal of fat from nibs.

2.8.description: Standards for various sweetening agents including sugars, honey, jaggery, and artificial sweeteners.

2.8.1.description: Includes varieties such as Plantation White Sugar, Refined Sugar, Khandsari Sugar, Bura Sugar, Cube Sugar, and Icing Sugar.

2.8.2.description: Candy product obtained from any kind of sugar or palmyrah juice.

2.8.3.description: Natural sweet substance produced by honey bees, including various floral sources, Bees Wax, and Royal Jelly.

2.8.4.description: Product obtained by boiling or processing juice extracted from specific palms or sugarcane.

2.8.5.description: White or light cream granular powder, odourless and having a sweet taste.

2.8.6.description: Golden yellow syrup obtained by inversion of sugar, free from crystallization.

2.8.7.description: Coarse or fine, white to creamish white powder with a sweet taste and bland flavour.

2.8.8.description: White crystals or crystalline powder that is intensely sweet to taste.

2.8.9.description: Material slightly soluble in water and Methanol containing not less than 98 percent Aspertame on dried basis.

2.8.10.description: Odourless, white crystalline powder having intensely sweet taste, also known as Acesulfame-K.

2.8.11.description: White to off-white, odourless, crystalline powder having a sweet taste.

2.8.12.description: White crystals or white crystalline powder with an intensely sweet taste.

2.9.category_name: SALT, SPICES, CONDIMENTS AND RELATED PRODUCTS
2.9.description: Standards for salt, spices, condiments, and related products including permissible limits for extraneous matter and microbiological requirements.

2.9.1.description: Mericarps of nearly mature fruit of Carum carvi L. or Carum bulbocastanum, available in whole or powder form.

2.9.2.description: Dried capsules or seeds of Elettaria cardamomum or Amomum subulatum, covering small and large varieties in whole, seed, or powder form.

2.9.3.description: Dried ripe fruits or pods of Capsicum annum L and Capsicum frutescens L, available as whole or powder.

2.9.4.description: Inner bark of trunks or branches of Cinnamomum Zeylanicum Blume, available as whole or powder.

2.9.5.description: Bark of trees of various Cinnamomum species including Cinnamomum Cassia, available as whole or powder.

2.9.6.description: Dried unopened flower buds of Eugenia Caryophyllus, available as whole or powder.

2.9.7.description: Dried mature fruits (seeds) of Coriandrum sativum L., available as whole or powder.

2.9.8.description: Dried mature seeds of Cuminum cyminum L. (White) or Nigella sativa L. (Black), available as whole or powder.

2.9.9.description: Dried ripe fruit of Foeniculum vulgare P. Miller Var. Vulgare, available as whole or powder.
ingredients: Foeniculum vulgare P. Miller Var. Vulgare

2.9.10.description: Dried mature seeds of Trigonella foenum graecum L., available as whole or powder.

2.9.11.description: Dried rhizome of Zingiber officinale Roscoe, available as whole or powder, bleached or unbleached.

2.9.12.description: Dried coat or aril of the seed of Myristica fragrans Houttuyn, available as whole or powder.

2.9.13.description: Dried, clean mature seeds of various Brassica species, available as whole or powder.

2.9.14.description: Dried seed (kernel) of Myristica fragrans Houttuyn, available as whole or powder.

2.9.15.description: Berries of Piper nigrum L. processed into black, white, or green pepper forms, whole, cracked, or ground.

2.9.16.description: Dried mature seeds of Papaver somniferum L.

2.9.17.description: Dried stigmas or tops of styles of Crocus Sativus Linnaeus, available as whole or powder.

2.9.18.description: Primary or secondary rhizomes of Curcuma Longa L., available as whole or powder.

2.9.19.description: Powder obtained by grinding a mixture of specific aromatic herbs and spices, optionally with salt and starch.

2.9.20.description: Mixture of clean, dried and sound aromatic herbs and spices, whole or powder, with permitted ingredients.

2.9.21.description: Dried and mature fruit of Pimpinella anisum L.

2.9.22.description: Dried ripe fruits (seeds) of Trachyspermum ammi. L Sprague.

2.9.23.description: Dried wholesome, edible part of raw mango fruit with or without outer skin.

2.9.24.description: Powder obtained by grinding dried mango slices.

2.9.26.description: Dried or dehydrated cloves of Allium sativum L., whole, sliced, or powdered.

2.9.27.description: Dried ripe fruits (seeds) of Apium graveoleans L.

2.9.28.description: Product obtained by removal of most moisture from sound bulbs of Allium cepa L.

2.9.29.description: Oleogumresin obtained from the rhizome and roots of Ferula species, including Compounded Asafoetida (Bandhani Hing).

2.9.30.description: Crystalline solid sodium chloride, including Iron Fortified Iodized Salt standards.

2.9.31.description: Product intended to enhance flavour containing a mixture of ingredients like spices, salts, and extracts.

2.9.32.description: Volatile and non-volatile constituents of spices or herbs obtained by solvent extraction.

2.9.33.description: Dried leaves of the tree Cinnamomum tamala, Nees and Eberm.

2.9.34.description: Dried mature fruit of the tree Illicium verum Hook.

2.9.35.description: Leaves of the Origanum genus excluding Origanum majorana, available as whole or powder.

2.9.36.description: Dried berry of Pimenta dioica (L.) Merr., available as whole or powder.

2.9.37.category_name: Dried Laurel or Dried Bay Leaf
2.9.37.description: Dried leaf of the tree Laurus nobilis L., available as whole or powder.

2.9.38.description: Dried leaves or broken/crushed leaves of Mentha spicata Linnaeus.

2.9.39.description: Dried leaves of the species Rosmarinus officinalis L.

2.9.40.description: Product prepared from leaves/flowers of Thymus spp., available as whole, crushed, or ground.

2.9.41.description: Leaves of the species Salvia officinalis L., collected before flowering and dried.

2.9.42.description: Dried leaves obtained from the species Ocimum basilicum L.

2.9.43.description: Dried leaves obtained from the species Origanum majorana L.

2.9.44.description: Whole, cut, or broken dried leaves of Petroselinum crispum.

2.9.45.description: Dried leaves obtained from perennial plant Artemisia dracunculus Linnaeus, whole or powder.

2.10.category_name: BEVERAGES, (Other than Dairy and Fruits & Vegetables based)
2.10.description: Category covering beverages excluding dairy and fruit/vegetable based products, including tea, coffee, water, and mixtures.

2.10.1.description: Products derived from Camellia sinensis including black, oolong, green, and instant tea varieties.

2.10.1.1.description: Tea obtained exclusively from the leaves, buds, and tender stems of Camellia sinensis.
ingredients: leaves, buds and tender stems of Camellia sinensis

2.10.1.2.description: Tea derived exclusively from plants grown in Kangra and Mandi valleys of Himachal Pradesh.
ingredients: leaves, buds and tender stems of Camellia sinensis

2.10.1.3.description: Product derived exclusively from Camellia sinensis by enzyme inactivation, rolling, or comminution and drying.
ingredients: leaves, buds and tender stems of Camellia sinensis

2.10.1.4.description: Dried water-dispersible solids obtained by aqueous extraction of Camellia sinensis.
ingredients: leaves, buds and stems of Camellia sinensis

2.10.2.description: Coffee products including green, roasted, ground, soluble, and decaffeinated varieties.

2.10.2.1.description: Dried seeds of Coffea species with husks removed.

2.10.2.2.description: Properly cleaned green coffee roasted to a brown colour with characteristic aroma.

2.10.2.3.description: Powdered products obtained from roasted coffee only, free from husk.

2.10.2.4.description: Roasted coffee from which nearly all caffeine has been removed.

2.10.2.5.description: Free flowing powder or agglomerated granules obtained from freshly roasted and ground pure coffee beans.
ingredients: freshly roasted and ground pure coffee beans

2.10.2.6.description: Soluble coffee powder from which most of the caffeine has been removed.
ingredients: freshly roasted and ground pure coffee beans

2.10.3.description: Roasted chicory powder obtained from roots of Chicorium intybus Lin.

2.10.3.1.description: Roasted chicory powder obtained by roasting and grinding cleaned and dried roots.

2.10.4.description: Mixtures of coffee and chicory in roasted/ground or instant forms.

2.10.4.1.description: Product prepared by mixing roasted and ground coffee and roasted and ground chicory.

2.10.4.2.description: Soluble product manufactured from roasted and ground coffee and roasted and ground chicory.

2.10.4.3.category_name: Decaffeinated Roasted and Ground coffee-chicory mixture
2.10.4.3.description: Mixture of decaffeinated roasted and ground coffee and roasted and ground chicory.

2.10.4.4.description: Soluble mixture manufactured from decaffeinated roasted and ground coffee and roasted and ground chicory.

2.10.6.description: Non-alcoholic beverages including carbonated water, caffeinated drinks, and coconut neera.

2.10.6.1.description: Water impregnated with carbon dioxide under pressure with optional sweeteners and additives.
ingredients: salts of sodium, calcium and magnesium

2.10.6.2.description: Carbonated or non-carbonated beverages containing 145 to 300 mg/L of total caffeine.

2.10.6.3.description: Beverages containing water without added carbon dioxide, with optional sugar, fruit, or herbs.

2.10.6.4.description: Phloem sap from the unopened coconut spadix, fresh or processed.

2.10.7.description: Packaged natural mineral waters obtained from underground sources.

2.10.7.1.description: Water clearly distinguished from ordinary drinking water by mineral content and underground source.

2.10.7.2.description: Water derived from an underground formation flowing naturally to the surface.

2.10.8.category_name: Packaged Drinking Water (other than Mineral Water)
2.10.8.description: Water likely to be used for human consumption, offered or sold in packaged form.

2.10.9.description: Water other than packaged drinking water and natural mineral water offered through vending machines.

2.11.category_name: Other food product and ingredients Regulations
2.11.description: Regulations covering miscellaneous food products and ingredients including baking powder, catechu, gelatin, silver leaf, pan masala, and special dietary foods.

2.11.1.description: A combination capable of yielding carbon dioxide under baking conditions, consisting of sodium bicarbonate, acid-reacting material, and starch.
ingredients: Mono calcium phosphate mono hydrate INS 341 (ii)

2.11.2.description: Dried aqueous extract prepared from the heart-wood of Acacia Catechu, free from infestation and impurities.

2.11.3.description: Purified product obtained by partial hydrolysis of collagen derived from the skin, white connective tissues, and bones of animals.

2.11.4.description: Food grade silver in the form of sheets of uniform thickness, free from creases, folds, and animal origin material.

2.11.5.description: Food generally taken as such or in conjunction with Pan, containing specific aromatic and sweetening ingredients.

2.11.6.description: This regulation section has been omitted.

2.11.7.description: Powder obtained from the roasted pods of carob (Ceratonia Siliqua), free from husk and artificial additives.

2.11.8.description: Glucose polymer of natural origin obtained by dextrinification of acidified starch.

2.11.9.category_name: Special dietary food with low sodium content
2.11.9.description: Food whose special dietary value results from the reduction, restriction, or removal of sodium.

2.12.description: Food articles not standardised under specific regulations but made using permitted ingredients and additives.

2.12.1.description: Non-standardised food articles excluding novel foods, special dietary uses, and functional foods.
ingredients: Ingredients in Indian Food Composition Tables (IFCT) 2017

2.13.description: Regulations governing the processing of food and allied products using ionizing radiation.

2.13.1.description: Specifies permissible radiation dose limits for defined classes of food products and allied items.

2.13.2.description: Mandates that irradiation facilities must be approved and licensed under Atomic Energy Rules.

2.13.3.category_name: Restrictions on Radiation Processing of Food
2.13.3.description: Sets operational restrictions including dose conformity, prevention of re-irradiation, and personnel qualifications.

2.13.4.category_name: Record of Radiation Processing of Food
2.13.4.description: Requires facilities to record details of all radiation treatments in accordance with rules.

2.13.5.description: Ensures processed food complies with the Food Safety and Standards Act and related regulations.

2.13.6.category_name: Storage and Sale of Radiation Processed Food
2.13.6.description: Prohibits the irradiation, storage, or transport for sale of food except as provided in the regulations.

2.13.7.category_name: Restriction on Sale of Radiation Processed Food
2.13.7.description: Mandates that radiation processed food must only be sold in pre-packaged conditions.

2.13.8.description: Specifies labelling requirements including the Radura logo, declaration of processing, and license details.

2.14.description: Food consisting of or made of ingredients containing rice, millets, ragi, pulses or legumes with gluten levels below 20 mg/kg.

2.15.category_name: Food specially processed to reduce gluten content to a level 20 -100mg/kg
2.15.description: This category was omitted vide Notification [F. No. 1-116/Scientific Committee/Notif.27/2010-FSSAI(E)] dated 04.03.2021 (Amendment no. 73).

2.16.description: Hulled, non-viable seeds obtained from Cannabis sativa or other indigenous Cannabis species complying with NDPS Act.

2.16.1.description: Standardized hemp seeds with specific limits for moisture, protein, fat, ash, and total THC.

2.16.2.description: Oil derived from hemp seeds with specific limits for free fatty acid, peroxide value, and total THC.

2.16.3.description: Solid product obtained after milling seeds to powder, with or without oil extraction.

2.16.4.description: Beverages containing hemp seeds subject to a maximum total THC limit of 0.2 mg/kg.

2.16.5.category_name: Other food containing hemp seed or seed products
2.16.5.description: Any other food products containing hemp components subject to a maximum total THC limit of 5 mg/kg.
